pages.name: Home
pages.description: Entry point to the catering website with quick access to key event types and tools, including featured packages and shortcuts into core flows like corporate lunches, weddings, parties, custom menus, and booking.
primary_functions: Highlight main services such as corporate catering, weddings, parties, coffee breaks, and custom menus
Provide prominent links to browse packages, build a custom menu, check availability, and request quotes
Show featured packages and shortcuts to common tasks like corporate lunches, birthday parties, and wedding dinners

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Corporate Events' highlight card or quick action
Click on 'Weddings' highlight card or quick action
Click on 'Parties & Birthdays' highlight card or quick action
Click on 'Coffee Breaks' highlight card or quick action
Click on a general 'Browse All Packages' call-to-action
Click on 'Build a Custom Dinner Menu' quick action
Click on 'Book Now' or 'Check Availability' quick action
Click on a generic 'Request a Quote' quick action not tied to a specific package
Click on a featured package card
Click on cart icon in the header

access_methods.description: Main site entry via 'Home' link in the header navigation
Users can land directly on the homepage via the root URL

pages.name: Packages Listing
pages.description: Unified listing page for all event catering packages with filters by event type, price, guests, and features, used as the entry point for corporate, wedding, party, and coffee break packages.
primary_functions: List catering packages for selected categoryId (e.g., corporate_events, weddings, parties_birthdays, coffee_breaks, all_events)
Filter packages by guest count or guest range to find options suitable for 25, 40, 50, 60, 80, or 120 guests
Filter by price per person (e.g., max $25 or $50) and by attributes such as meal type (lunch, dinner, buffet, coffee_break), course count (3_courses), and includes_dessert
Filter or tag packages by dietary options (e.g., vegetarian-friendly) when relevant
Sort packages by price (low to high or high to low) to help users pick mid-priced options
Open a specific package in the Package Detail page for further customization and actions

incoming_params.description: Package category filter; one of corporate_events, weddings, parties_birthdays, coffee_breaks, all_events. Set by header/footer navigation and internal links.
Optional initial guest count filter (e.g., 25, 40, 50, 60, 80, 120) used to pre-populate guest filters.
Optional maximum price-per-person used when sharing or reloading filtered results (e.g., 25 or 50).
Optional minimum price-per-person filter used in combination with pricePerPersonMax.
Optional filter flag to show only packages that include dessert (true/false), useful for birthday tasks.
Optional filter flag to restrict packages to vegetarian-friendly offerings.
Optional filter to restrict packages to an exact course count (e.g., 3 for 3-course wedding dinners).
Optional sort option; one of price_low_to_high, price_high_to_low, rating, name.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a package card or name in the listing
Click on header cart icon from the listing page
Click on breadcrumb or logo to return to the homepage

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Corporate Events', 'Weddings', 'Parties & Birthdays', 'Coffee Breaks', or 'All Packages' header links, each setting categoryId appropriately
Accessed via footer links for corporate events, weddings, parties & birthdays, and coffee breaks
Directly accessed via packages.html?categoryId=... with optional filter parameters

pages.name: Package Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific catering package including pricing, menu options, add-ons, and event date, used to configure corporate lunches, birthday parties, wedding dinners, coffee breaks, and general packages before adding to cart or requesting a quote.
primary_functions: Display full details of a selected package including description, included items, pricing per person, and total price
Allow users to set or update guest count (e.g., 25, 40, 50, 60, 80, 120) and automatically recalculate totals
Provide meal/menu customization where applicable, including selection of vegetarian dishes, course combinations, and buffet items
Offer an add-ons section for extras such as bar service, additional courses, or upgrades, allowing users to select exactly 2 add-ons for relevant tasks
Allow selection of an event date (e.g., three consecutive days for coffee breaks) so multiple dated line items can be added to the cart from the same package
Enable users to add the configured package to the cart as a line item with its guest count, date, and selected options

Provide a 'Request Quote' or similar action that passes the configured package, guest count, date, and add-ons to the Quote Request page when a quote is needed (e.g., wedding plated dinner with bar service)

incoming_params.description: Package ID to display; required. Usually comes from the Packages Listing or a featured package on the homepage.
Optional initial guest count to pre-fill the guest selector (e.g., 25, 40, 60, 120).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'View Cart' or 'Go to Cart' after successfully adding the configured package via addPackageToCart
Click on 'Request Quote' button for the configured package

access_methods.description: Accessed via package_detail.html?id=PACKAGE_ID with optional guestCount parameter
Reached by clicking on a package from the Packages Listing or a featured package on the homepage

pages.name: Cart & Checkout
pages.description: Cart and order summary page showing all selected packages and custom menus, with promo code and totals; used to inspect items like corporate lunches, birthday packages, custom menus, and coffee breaks and to apply discounts before finalizing.
primary_functions: List all line items including standard packages, custom menus, and dated coffee break orders with their guest counts, event dates, and selected options
Show per-person pricing, line-item subtotals, and overall totals to verify budgets (e.g., ensuring totals stay under $2,600 after discount)
Allow users to remove items or adjust quantities/guest counts before finalizing their selection if needed
Provide a promo/discount code field where users can enter codes such as SAVE15 and apply a 15% discount to the order
Display an updated order summary section after applying discounts, showing final discounted totals
Serve as the summary destination after adding items from package details or custom menu builder so users can confirm that their chosen package or menu appears as a single line item

outgoing_connections.trigger: Optional 'Check Date Availability' call-to-action from the cart (if provided by implementation)
Click on 'Continue Browsing' to find more packages

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Cart' link in the header navigation
Linked from 'View Cart' buttons after adding items on other pages

pages.name: Availability & Booking
pages.description: Booking calendar and availability checker for selecting event dates, times, and guest counts, used to choose slots such as the earliest available Saturday evening in July 2025.
primary_functions: Provide a calendar with month and year navigation to view future availability (e.g., July 2025)
Indicate available dates and time slots, including visual markers for days with evening availability
Allow users to select a specific date (such as the earliest available Saturday in July 2025) and an evening time slot (e.g., 5:00 PM or later)
Include a guest count field to set the intended number of guests (e.g., 80) for the booking
Pass the selected date, time, and guest count forward to the Booking Details page when the user clicks Continue or Next

incoming_params.description: Optional month (1-12) to pre-position the calendar, e.g., 7 for July.
Optional 4-digit year to pre-position the calendar, e.g., 2025.

outgoing_connections.trigger: After selecting an available slot and entering guest count, clicking 'Continue' which calls initBookingFromAvailabilitySlot and navigates to booking details

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Book Now' header link
Accessed from homepage quick actions or from other content blocks linking to availability.html
May be accessed with specific month/year in the query string to show a target month

pages.name: Booking Details
pages.description: Form page to collect event details and contact information for a selected booking slot, following the calendar selection.
primary_functions: Display a summary of the selected date, time, and guest count from the availability step for confirmation
Collect event-specific information such as event name (e.g., 'Quarterly Celebration') and event type (corporate or social)
Collect contact details such as organizer name and phone number (and optionally email) needed to finalize the booking
Allow users to proceed to the Booking Summary page via a Continue or Review Booking button

incoming_params.description: Booking draft ID created from the selected availability slot; used to load slot details for review.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Continue' or 'Review Booking' after submitting the event details form via submitBookingDetails

access_methods.description: Accessed via booking_details.html?bookingId=... after selecting a slot on the availability page
Reached only after completing the availability step and clicking 'Continue'

pages.name: Booking Summary
pages.description: Confirmation-style summary page for the selected booking slot and event details, used as the final non-payment step of the booking flow.
primary_functions: Summarize all booking details including date, time, guest count, event name, event type, and contact information
Provide a final on-screen confirmation that the chosen Saturday evening slot for the specified number of guests has been captured
Optionally offer a final submit or confirm action (if required in implementation) or simply act as the final step in the booking flow for user review

incoming_params.description: ID of the booking whose summary should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Optional 'Book Another Date' or 'Back to Calendar' action

access_methods.description: Accessed via booking_summary.html?bookingId=... as the final step of the booking flow
Reached after submitting the Booking Details form

pages.name: Custom Menu Builder
pages.description: Interactive builder to create custom menus (e.g., dinner) with selectable dishes and budget controls, supporting configuration of items and per-person pricing before adding to the cart.
primary_functions: Allow users to choose a meal type (e.g., Dinner) as the basis for the custom menu
Provide an optional budget field to set a maximum price per person (e.g., $55) and track the menu cost against this limit
List appetizer options with 'Add' or '+' actions so users can choose 2 different appetizers
List main course options with 'Add' or '+' actions so users can choose 2 different mains
List dessert options with 'Add' or '+' actions so users can choose 1 dessert item
Display the current price per person as items are added or removed and allow users to remove or replace dishes to keep the per-person price at or below the set budget
Provide a guest count field for entering a number between 20 and 30 guests

Offer a primary action such as 'Add Menu to Cart' or 'Review Menu' that creates a single cart line item representing the custom dinner menu and navigates the user to the Cart & Checkout page to see the summary

incoming_params.description: Optional meal type, typically 'dinner' for the custom dinner builder. Defaults to 'dinner' if not provided.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Add Menu to Cart' or 'View Summary' after calculating and accepting the custom menu price

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Custom Menu' header link
Reached from homepage quick actions linking to the custom menu builder
May include mealType in query string to pre-select the type of custom menu

pages.name: Full Menu (Dishes Listing)
pages.description: Browseable list of individual dishes with filters for dietary needs and ratings, used to explore items and to start gluten-free tasting list flows.
primary_functions: Display all individual dishes (appetizers, mains, desserts) available for browsing outside of packaged menus
Provide filters for dietary preferences such as a 'Gluten-Free' option to limit results to gluten-free dishes
Provide a rating filter or sort option to show only dishes rated 4+ stars
Allow users to filter by dish type (e.g., savory vs dessert) to help identify suitable appetizers/mains versus dessert items
Enable navigation to a specific dishs detail page for more information and to add items to a tasting list

incoming_params.description: Determines list mode; currently expected to be 'all_items' and set by the 'Full Menu' header link.
Optional dietary filter query string (e.g., 'gluten_free', 'vegetarian', 'vegan') used to persist or share filtered states.
Optional minimum rating (e.g., 4) used to show only highly rated dishes.
Optional filter for dish type such as 'appetizer', 'main', or 'dessert'.
Optional free-text search term used to narrow dish results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a dish card or title in the full menu listing
Click on 'View Tasting List' (or header icon) while browsing dishes

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Full Menu' header navigation link which sets view_type=all_items
Can be accessed directly with filters encoded in query parameters

pages.name: Dish Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single dish including rating, dietary tags, and tasting list controls, supporting flows like building a gluten-free tasting list with highly rated savory and dessert items.
primary_functions: Show detailed information about a selected dish including ingredients, dietary tags (e.g., gluten-free), and rating
Indicate the dish type (e.g., appetizer, main, dessert) so users can differentiate savory items from desserts
Allow users to add the dish to their tasting list or favorites via an 'Add to Tasting List' button or heart icon
Support returning to the filtered dish list or navigating elsewhere after adding items to the tasting list

incoming_params.description: ID of the dish to display; set when clicking a dish from the full menu or tasting list.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'View Tasting List' or tasting icon after adding the dish to the tasting list
Click on 'Back to Full Menu' link

access_methods.description: Accessed via dish_detail.html?dishId=... when user selects a dish
Reached via direct clicks from full menu or tasting list items

pages.name: Tasting List / Favorites
pages.description: Page displaying the user’s saved tasting set or favorites list of individual dishes, including gluten-free tasting collections.
primary_functions: List all dishes that the user has added to their tasting list, including savory and dessert items
Group the 3 gluten-free tasting items (including at least 1 dessert) together as a visible tasting set for review
Allow users to remove items from the tasting list if they change their selections
Provide quick access back to dish or menu browsing if the user wants to refine or expand their tasting set

incoming_params.description: Type of list to display, either 'tasting' or 'favorites'. Defaults to 'tasting' when not provided.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Browse More Dishes' from the tasting list page
Click on a dish name or image within the tasting list

access_methods.description: Accessed from the 'Tasting List' header link or icon
May include listType parameter to distinguish between tasting and favorites lists

pages.name: Quote Request
pages.description: Form for requesting a detailed catering quote for a configured package on a specific date, typically reached from a package (e.g., a wedding plated dinner with bar service).
primary_functions: Display a summary of the selected package, guest count, and any chosen add-ons (e.g., standard bar service) passed from the Package Detail page
Provide a date picker to select an event date (e.g., a specific wedding date)
Collect contact information such as name (e.g., Alex Taylor), email, phone, and any additional notes or requirements
Allow users to submit the quote request via a 'Request Quote' or 'Submit' button and show an inline confirmation that the request was received

incoming_params.description: ID of the package for which the quote is being requested; required for package-specific quote flows.
Guest count selected on the package detail page (e.g., 120 for a wedding).
Comma-separated list of add-on IDs (e.g., bar service) selected with the package.
Optional ISO-like date string representing the event date pre-selected on the package page.

access_methods.description: Accessed via quote_request.html?packageId=... with optional guest/add-on/date parameters
Reached by clicking 'Request Quote' from a package detail or generic quote CTAs on the site

pages.name: About
pages.description: Information about the catering business, its story, and service philosophy, reassuring clients about expertise across corporate events, weddings, parties, and coffee breaks.
primary_functions: Introduce the company, its history, and areas of specialization (corporate events, weddings, parties, etc.)
Highlight quality standards, catering experience, and any awards or certifications
Reassure potential clients about reliability, service levels, and flexibility

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Contact Us' link or button from the About page
Click on 'Explore Our Menus' or similar action

access_methods.description: Accessed from the 'About' link in the site footer
Can be accessed directly via about.html URL

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact information and simple inquiry form for general questions, not tied to a specific package or quote.
primary_functions: Provide contact details such as phone numbers, email addresses, and business address
Offer a simple contact form for general inquiries that are not tied to specific packages or quote requests
Indicate business hours and expected response times for messages

incoming_params.description: Optional subject line pre-fill for the contact form (e.g., 'Booking Question', 'General Inquiry').

access_methods.description: Accessed from the 'Contact' link in the footer
May include a pre-filled subject via the subject query parameter

pages.name: FAQ
pages.description: Frequently asked questions about services, booking, pricing, and policies, helping users understand how ordering, dietary accommodations, and scheduling work.
primary_functions: Answer common questions about ordering, minimum guest counts, lead time, and service areas
Explain how pricing works, including per-person pricing and how add-ons and discounts are applied
Clarify procedures for booking changes, cancellations, and dietary accommodations

incoming_params.description: Optional FAQ category filter such as 'ordering', 'pricing', 'dietary', or 'booking'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Still have questions? Contact us' from the FAQ page

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'FAQ' link in the footer
Can be filtered by FAQ category via the category query parameter

pages.name: Policies
pages.description: Policy information including privacy, terms of service, and cancellation/refund rules, consolidating multiple policy documents in a single page with internal navigation.
primary_functions: Describe terms and conditions for using the catering services and website
Present privacy policy details including handling of customer data and quote requests
Outline payment, cancellation, and refund policies relevant to event bookings and catering orders

incoming_params.description: Optional specific policy page ID to scroll to or highlight within the Policies page.
Optional policy type such as 'privacy_policy', 'terms_of_service', or 'cancellation_policy' to focus on a given policy.

access_methods.description: Accessed from the 'Policies' link in the footer
Can be deep-linked using policyId or type query parameters

header_links.description: Homepage with quick access to all main catering sections
Corporate and business catering packages including lunches and coffee breaks
Wedding catering packages such as plated dinners and receptions
Social event and birthday party catering packages
Coffee break and meeting catering packages
Browse all catering packages across event types
Build-your-own custom menu for dinners and other meals
Browse all individual dishes with filters for dietary needs and ratings
Check date availability and start a booking for an event
View your saved tasting list or favorites
View and manage selected packages, custom menus, and apply promo codes

footer_links.description: Learn more about the catering company
Get in touch for general questions or assistance
Frequently asked questions about services and bookings
Privacy, terms of service, and cancellation policies
Social and birthday party catering overview
Coffee break and meeting catering overview
